abstract: This research investigates how discipline and religious values synergize to foster resilience and responsibility among students at SMP Nidaul Quran in Sarolangun Regency. Adopting a qualitative case study design, the study gathered data through in-depth interviews, field observations, and thematic document analysis. The findings reveal that discipline is a fundamental pillar of character formation, particularly when integrated into a pedagogic framework centered on moral development. By successfully aligning disciplinary standards with core religious tenets, SMP Nidaul Quran cultivates a student body characterized by psychological fortitude and accountability. Key drivers of this success include the role of teachers as moral exemplars and the proactive involvement of parents. Ultimately, this research underscores that the integration of religious values transcends academic achievement, instilling a deep sense of integrity. This holistic collaboration between educators, families, and spiritual values offers a robust model for other Islamic institutions, demonstrating that education is most effective when it shapes the heart and soul, preparing students to navigate life’s challenges with steadfastness.
